contextPack.industry_context: Home gardening, particularly growing tomatoes, has seen increased interest as people seek sustainable and self-sufficient food sources. The popularity of home gardening surged during the COVID-19 pandemic as individuals spent more time at home and pursued gardening as a hobby.

terminology_glossary.Transplanting: Moving a plant from one location to another, typically from a pot to the ground in a garden.
terminology_glossary.Pruning: The selective removal of certain parts of a plant, such as branches or stems, to improve its health and growth.
terminology_glossary.Mulching: The practice of covering the soil with a layer of material to retain moisture and suppress weeds.
terminology_glossary.Heirloom Tomatoes: Varieties of tomatoes that are open-pollinated and have been passed down through generations.
terminology_glossary.Blossom End Rot: A common disorder in tomatoes characterized by a dark, sunken spot on the blossom end of the fruit, usually caused by calcium deficiency.

typical_workflows.traditional_process: Traditional tomato growing involves selecting a suitable variety, preparing the soil, planting seeds or seedlings, providing regular watering and fertilization, supporting plants with stakes or cages, and monitoring for pests and diseases. An optimized process may include using raised beds for better drainage, employing drip irrigation systems for consistent moisture, and using organic fertilizers and pest control methods.

uncertainties: [uncertain: Specific company and product details not provided]

2.aiVersion:
[
  {
    "screen_number": 1,
    "purpose": "Goal Statement - What we'll accomplish",
    "rough_duration": 6,
    "screen_type": "slides/text overlay",
    "voiceover_text": "Today, you'll learn how to grow delicious tomatoes right in your backyard.",
    "visual_direction": "Text overlay with an image of a thriving tomato plant in a backyard garden.",
    "notes": "Set the goal of the video clearly and attract viewers interested in home gardening."
  },
  {
    "screen_number": 2,
    "purpose": "Audience Baseline - Increased interest in home gardening",
    "rough_duration": 7,
    "screen_type": "stock video",
    "voiceover_text": "Home gardening has surged in popularity as people seek sustainability and self-sufficiency.",
    "visual_direction": "Footage of various home gardens with people tending to their plants, showcasing diversity and sustainability.",
    "notes": "Highlight the rising trend and connect with the audience's motivations."
  },
  {
    "screen_number": 3,
    "purpose": "Setup/Prerequisites - Soil preparation",
    "rough_duration": 10,
    "screen_type": "slides/text overlay",
    "voiceover_text": "Begin with soil preparation. Ensure the soil is rich, well-drained, and slightly acidic for optimal growth.",
    "visual_direction": "Diagram illustrating soil composition with labels for nutrients, drainage, and pH balance.",
    "notes": "Introduce soil preparation as the first crucial step, using visual aids to explain components."
  },
  {
    "screen_number": 4,
    "purpose": "Step 1 - Selecting the right tomato variety",
    "rough_duration": 8,
    "screen_type": "slides/text overlay",
    "voiceover_text": "Choose a tomato variety that suits your climate and taste preferences.",
    "visual_direction": "List of popular tomato varieties with images and descriptions, highlighting climate suitability.",
    "notes": "Provide guidance on choosing the right tomato type for different conditions."
  },
  {
    "screen_number": 5,
    "purpose": "Step 2 - Planting seeds or seedlings",
    "rough_duration": 9,
    "screen_type": "screencast",
    "voiceover_text": "Plant seeds or seedlings in the prepared soil, keeping them about 2-3 feet apart.",
    "visual_direction": "Demonstration of planting seeds or seedlings in a garden bed, marking spacing with a ruler.",
    "notes": "Focus on proper planting technique and spacing for healthy growth."
  },
  {
    "screen_number": 6,
    "purpose": "Step 3 - Watering and fertilization",
    "rough_duration": 8,
    "screen_type": "screencast",
    "voiceover_text": "Water regularly, ensuring the soil remains moist, and fertilize every few weeks.",
    "visual_direction": "Close-up of watering a tomato plant with a watering can and applying fertilizer.",
    "notes": "Stress the importance of consistent watering and fertilization."
  },
  {
    "screen_number": 7,
    "purpose": "Step 4 - Supporting plants",
    "rough_duration": 7,
    "screen_type": "screencast",
    "voiceover_text": "Use stakes or cages to support your tomato plants as they grow taller.",
    "visual_direction": "Demonstration of installing stakes and cages around young tomato plants.",
    "notes": "Emphasize the need for structural support to prevent plants from falling over."
  },
  {
    "screen_number": 8,
    "purpose": "Step 5 - Monitoring for pests and diseases",
    "rough_duration": 8,
    "screen_type": "slides/text overlay",
    "voiceover_text": "Keep an eye out for common pests and diseases, and take action immediately to protect your plants.",
    "visual_direction": "Images of common tomato pests and diseases with prevention tips.",
    "notes": "Provide visual cues and tips for pest and disease management."
  },
  {
    "screen_number": 9,
    "purpose": "Common Pitfalls - Avoiding mistakes",
    "rough_duration": 8,
    "screen_type": "slides/text overlay",
    "voiceover_text": "Avoid overwatering, overcrowding, and neglecting pest control to ensure healthy plants.",
    "visual_direction": "Checklist of common mistakes with visual examples of issues caused by these mistakes.",
    "notes": "Highlight common errors and how to avoid them in a concise format."
  },
  {
    "screen_number": 10,
    "purpose": "Result/Verification - Confirm success",
    "rough_duration": 7,
    "screen_type": "stock video",
    "voiceover_text": "With care and patience, you'll soon enjoy a bountiful harvest of homegrown tomatoes.",
    "visual_direction": "Footage of a gardener picking ripe tomatoes from lush plants and displaying the harvest.",
    "notes": "Wrap up with a positive, encouraging visual of successful tomato gardening."
  }
]
